revision.text:
'''下毛野 虫麻呂'''（しもつけの の むしまろ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[奈良時代]]前紀の[[官人]]・[[学者]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[朝臣]]。[[官位]]は[[従五位|従五位上]]・[[式部省|式部]][[員外官|員外]]少輔・大学助教。
== 出自 == 下毛野虫麻呂は[[崇神天皇]]の皇子、[[豊城入彦命]]の後裔と称する一族で、[[下野国]]（下毛野国）の名を冠する氏族、[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]を出自とする。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」の下毛野国造では、[[仁徳天皇]]の代に[[毛野国]]を分けて上下とし、豊城命の4世の孫の[[奈良別]]を初めて[[国造]]としたとあり、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』「左京皇別」下の大網公氏の項目では、「[[上毛野氏|上毛野朝臣]]同祖、豊城入彦命六世孫下毛君奈良弟真若君之後也」とあり、下毛野氏が国造になっていたことが分かる。一族は[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）の[[八色の姓]]で[[朝臣]][[カバネ|姓]]になっている。 == 経歴 == [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）1月、[[正六位|正六位上]]から[[従五位|従五位下]]に昇叙。是の日、白い虹が南北に空をわたったという<ref>『続日本紀』元正天皇、養老4年正月11日条</ref>。
養老5年（[[721年]]）1月、[[呉粛胡明]]とともに、[[従五位下]]から従五位上に昇叙<ref>『続日本紀』元正天皇、養老5年正月5日条</ref>。同月、[[元正天皇]]が官人の中から学業に優れ人々の師範たるに堪える者を選んで褒賞を与えた際、[[文章道|文章]]に優れるとして、[[山田三方]]・[[紀清人]]・[[高丘河内|楽浪河内]]とともに、[[あしぎぬ|絁]]15疋・[[絹糸]]15絇・[[麻織物|麻布]]30端・[[鍬]]20口を与えられている<ref>『続日本紀』元正天皇、養老5年正月27日条</ref>。同年6月、[[式部省]]の[[員外官|員外]]少輔に任命されている<ref>『続日本紀』元正天皇、養老5年6月26日条</ref>。
史書に現れている経歴はここまでだが、このほか、[[経国集]]に[[対策]]文が2つ収録されており、『[[懐風藻]]』に[[長屋王]]の邸宅で行われた、とある秋に[[新羅]]の客を送る宴で詠まれた漢詩作品1首（五言[[律詩]]）が序文とともに採録されている。その目次によると、「従五位下」・「大学助教」、「年三十六」とあり、惜しくも若くしてなくなったことが分かる。 == 官歴 == 『[[六国史]]』による。 * [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）以前：[[正六位|正六位上]] * [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）1月5日：[[従五位|従五位下]]。 * [[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）1月5日：従五位上 * [[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）1月27日：[[あしぎぬ|絁]]15疋・[[絹糸]]15絇・[[麻織物|麻布]]30端・[[鍬]]20口を授与。 * [[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）6月26日：[[式部省]]の[[員外官|員外]]少輔に任官。 * [[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）以降：大学助教に任官。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *『[[続日本紀]]』2 新日本古典文学大系13 [[岩波書店]]、1990年 * [[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社学術文庫]]、1992年、ISBN 4061590308 *[[江口孝夫]]『懐風藻 全訳注』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、2000年 * [[坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|坂本太郎]]・[[平野邦雄]]監修『日本古代氏族人名辞典』[[吉川弘文館]]、1990年、ISBN 4642022430 *[[佐伯有清]]編『日本古代氏族事典』[[雄山閣]]、1994年
{{DEFAULTSORT:しもつけの の むしまろ}} [[Category:下毛野氏|むしまろ]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:奈良時代の学者]] [[Category:日本の漢詩人]] [[Category:7世紀生]] [[Category:7世紀没]]

'''下毛野 虫麻呂'''（しもつけの の むしまろ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[奈良時代]]前紀の[[官人]]・[[学者]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[朝臣]]。[[官位]]は[[従五位|従五位上]]・[[式部省|式部]][[員外官|員外]]少輔・大学助教。
== 出自 == 下毛野虫麻呂は[[崇神天皇]]の皇子、[[豊城入彦命]]の後裔と称する一族で、[[下野国]]（下毛野国）の名を冠する氏族、[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]を出自とする。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」の下毛野国造では、[[仁徳天皇]]の代に[[毛野国]]を分けて上下とし、豊城命の4世の孫の[[奈良別]]を初めて[[国造]]としたとあり、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』「左京皇別」下の大網公氏の項目では、「[[上毛野氏|上毛野朝臣]]同祖、豊城入彦命六世孫下毛君奈良弟真若君之後也」とあり、下毛野氏が国造になっていたことが分かる。一族は[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）の[[八色の姓]]で[[朝臣]][[カバネ|姓]]になっている。
== 経歴 == [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）1月、[[正六位|正六位上]]から[[従五位|従五位下]]に昇叙。是の日、白い虹が南北に空をわたったという<ref>『続日本紀』元正天皇、養老4年正月11日条</ref>。
養老5年（[[721年]]）1月、[[呉粛胡明]]とともに、[[従五位下]]から従五位上に昇叙<ref>『続日本紀』元正天皇、養老5年正月5日条</ref>。同月、[[元正天皇]]が官人の中から学業に優れ人々の師範たるに堪える者を選んで褒賞を与えた際、[[文章道|文章]]に優れるとして、[[山田三方]]・[[紀清人]]・[[高丘河内|楽浪河内]]とともに、[[あしぎぬ|絁]]15疋・[[絹糸]]15絇・[[麻織物|麻布]]30端・[[鍬]]20口を与えられている<ref>『続日本紀』元正天皇、養老5年正月27日条</ref>。同年6月、[[式部省]]の[[員外官|員外]]少輔に任命されている<ref>『続日本紀』元正天皇、養老5年6月26日条</ref>。
史書に現れている経歴はここまでだが、このほか、[[経国集]]に[[対策]]文が2つ収録されており、『[[懐風藻]]』に[[長屋王]]の邸宅で行われた、とある秋に[[新羅]]の客を送る宴で詠まれた漢詩作品1首（五言[[律詩]]）が序文とともに採録されているが、その目次によると、「従五位下」・「大学助教」、「年三十六」とある。
== 官歴 == 『[[六国史]]』による。 * [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）以前：[[正六位|正六位上]] * [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）1月5日：[[従五位|従五位下]]。 * [[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）1月5日：従五位上 * [[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）1月27日：[[あしぎぬ|絁]]15疋・[[絹糸]]15絇・[[麻織物|麻布]]30端・[[鍬]]20口を授与。 * [[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）6月26日：[[式部省]]の[[員外官|員外]]少輔に任官。 * [[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）以降：大学助教に任官。

'''下毛野 虫麻呂'''（しもつけの の むしまろ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[奈良時代]]前紀の[[官人]]・[[学者]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[朝臣]]。[[官位]]は[[従五位|従五位上]]・大学助教・[[式部省|式部]][[員外官|員外]]少輔。
== 出自 == 下毛野虫麻呂は[[崇神天皇]]の皇子、[[豊城入彦命]]の後裔と称する一族で、[[下野国]]（下毛野国）の名を冠する氏族、[[下毛野氏|下毛野君]]を出自とする。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」の「[[下毛野国造]]」では、[[仁徳天皇]]の代に[[毛野国]]を分けて上下とし、豊城命の4世の孫の[[奈良別]]を初めて[[国造]]としたとあり、『[[新撰姓氏録]]』「左京皇別」下の大網公氏の項目では、「[[上毛野氏|上毛野朝臣]]同祖、豊城入彦命六世孫下毛君奈良弟真若君之後也」とあり、下毛野氏が国造になっていたことが分かる。一族は[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）の[[八色の姓]]で[[朝臣]][[カバネ|姓]]になっている。
== 経歴 == [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）1月、[[正六位|正六位上]]から[[従五位|従五位下]]に昇叙。是の日、白い虹が南北に空をわたったという<ref>『続日本紀』元正天皇、養老4年正月11日条</ref>。
養老5年（[[721年]]）1月、[[呉粛胡明]]とともに、[[従五位下]]から従五位上に昇叙<ref>『続日本紀』元正天皇、養老5年正月5日条</ref>。同月、[[元正天皇]]が官人の中から学業に優れ人々の師範たるに堪える者を選んで褒賞を与えた際、[[文章道|文章]]に優れるとして、[[山田三方]]・[[紀清人]]・[[高丘河内|楽浪河内]]とともに、[[あしぎぬ|絁]]15疋・[[絹糸]]15絇・[[麻織物|麻布]]30端・[[鍬]]20口を与えられている<ref>『続日本紀』元正天皇、養老5年正月27日条</ref>。同年6月、[[式部省]]の[[員外官|員外]]少輔に任命されている<ref>『続日本紀』元正天皇、養老5年6月26日条</ref>。
史書に現れている経歴はここまでだが、このほか、[[経国集]]に[[対策]]文が2つ収録されており、『[[懐風藻]]』に[[長屋王]]の邸宅で行われた、養老4年の秋に[[新羅]]の客を送る宴で詠まれた漢詩作品1首（五言[[律詩]]）が序文とともに採録されているが、その目次によると、「従五位下」・「大学助教」、「年三十六」とある。36歳でなくなったものと見られる。
== 官歴 == 『[[六国史]]』による。 * [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）以前：[[正六位|正六位上]] * [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）1月5日：[[従五位|従五位下]]。 * 同年月日不明：大学助教に任官。 * [[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）1月5日：従五位上 * 同年1月27日：[[あしぎぬ|絁]]15疋・[[絹糸]]15絇・[[麻織物|麻布]]30端・[[鍬]]20口を授与。 * 同年6月26日：[[式部省]]の[[員外官|員外]]少輔に任官。

== 参考文献 == *『[[続日本紀]]』2 新日本古典文学大系13 [[岩波書店]]、1990年 * [[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社学術文庫]]、1992年、ISBN 4061590308 *[[江口孝夫]]『懐風藻 全訳注』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、2000年 * [[坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|坂本太郎]]・[[平野邦雄]]監修『日本古代氏族人名辞典』[[吉川弘文館]]、1990年、ISBN 4642022430 *[[佐伯有清]]編『日本古代氏族事典』[[雄山閣]]、1994年

revision.comment: 出自セクション除去（[[下毛野氏]]と重複）。その他整理
revision.text:
'''下毛野 虫麻呂'''（しもつけの の むしまろ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[奈良時代]]前紀の[[貴族]]・[[文人]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[朝臣]]。[[参議]]・[[下毛野古麻呂]]の近親か。[[官位]]は[[従五位|従五位上]]・[[式部省|式部員外少輔]]。
== 経歴 == [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）[[従五位|従五位下]]に[[叙爵]]。翌養老5年（[[721年]]）[[呉粛胡明]]とともに従五位上に昇叙。さらに同月に[[元正天皇]]が官人の中から学業に優れ人々の師範たるに堪える者を選んで褒賞を与えた際、[[文章道|文章]]に優れるとして、[[山田三方]]・[[紀清人]]・[[高丘河内|楽浪河内]]とともに、[[あしぎぬ|絁]]15疋・[[絹糸]]15絇・[[麻織物|麻布]]30端・[[鍬]]20口を与えられている。同年6月には[[式部省|式部員外少輔]]に任官している。
史書に現れている経歴はここまでだが、このほか、[[経国集]]に[[対策]]文が2つ収録されている。また、『[[懐風藻]]』に[[長屋王]]の邸宅で行われた、養老4年の秋に[[新羅]]の客を送る宴で詠まれた漢詩作品1首（五言[[律詩]]）が序文とともに採録されているが、その目次によると官位は従五位下・大学助教で、年三十六とある。36歳で没したと見られる。
== 官歴 == 注記のないものは『[[続日本紀]]』による。 * 時期不詳：[[正六位|正六位上]] * [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）正月11日：[[従五位|従五位下]]。秋：見[[大学寮|大学助教]]<ref>『懐風藻』</ref> * [[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）正月5日：従五位上。正月27日：賜[[あしぎぬ|絁]]15疋・[[絹糸]]15絇・[[麻織物|麻布]]30端・[[鍬]]20口。6月26日：[[式部省|式部員外少輔]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *『[[続日本紀]]』2 新日本古典文学大系13 [[岩波書店]]、1990年 * [[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社学術文庫]]、1992年、ISBN 4061590308 *[[江口孝夫]]『懐風藻 全訳注』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、2000年 * [[坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|坂本太郎]]・[[平野邦雄]]監修『日本古代氏族人名辞典』[[吉川弘文館]]、1990年、ISBN 4642022430 *[[佐伯有清]]編『日本古代氏族事典』[[雄山閣]]、1994年
{{DEFAULTSORT:しもつけの の むしまろ}} [[Category:下毛野氏|むしまろ]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:奈良時代の学者]] [[Category:日本の漢詩人]] [[Category:7世紀生]] [[Category:8世紀没]]

'''下毛野 虫麻呂'''（しもつけの の むしまろ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[奈良時代]]前紀の[[貴族]]・[[文人]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[朝臣]]。[[参議]]・[[下毛野古麻呂]]の近親か。[[官位]]は[[従五位|従五位上]]・[[式部省|式部員外少輔]]。
== 経歴 == [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）[[従五位|従五位下]]に[[叙爵]]する。翌養老5年（[[721年]]）[[呉粛胡明]]とともに従五位上に昇叙。さらに同月に[[元正天皇]]が官人の中から学業に優れ人々の師範たるに堪える者を選んで褒賞を与えた際、[[文章道|文章]]に優れるとして、[[山田三方]]・[[紀清人]]・[[高丘河内|楽浪河内]]とともに、[[あしぎぬ|絁]]15疋・[[絹糸]]15絇・[[麻織物|麻布]]30端・[[鍬]]20口を与えられている。同年6月には[[式部省|式部員外少輔]]に任官している。
このほか、『[[経国集]]』に[[対策]]文が2つ収録されている。また、『[[懐風藻]]』に養老4年の秋に[[長屋王]]の邸宅で行われた[[新羅]]の客を送る宴で詠まれた[[漢詩]]作品1首（五言[[律詩]]）が序文とともに採録されているが、その目次によると[[官位]]は従五位下・大学助教で、年三十六とある。36歳で没したと見られる。
== 官歴 == 注記のないものは『[[続日本紀]]』による。 * 時期不詳：[[正六位|正六位上]] * [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）正月11日：[[従五位|従五位下]]。秋：見[[大学寮|大学助教]]<ref>『懐風藻』</ref> * [[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）正月5日：従五位上。正月27日：賜[[あしぎぬ|絁]]15疋・[[絹糸]]15絇・[[麻織物|麻布]]30端・[[鍬]]20口。6月26日：[[式部省|式部員外少輔]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *『続日本紀 2 （新日本古典文学大系13）』[[岩波書店]]、1990年 *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年、ISBN 4061590308 *[[江口孝夫]]『懐風藻 全訳注』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、2000年 * [[坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|坂本太郎]]・[[平野邦雄]]監修『日本古代氏族人名辞典』[[吉川弘文館]]、1990年、ISBN 4642022430

```wikitext '''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|en|ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|en|ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|en|ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|正六位上]] * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|従五位下]]. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上]]. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}
== References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.
[[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Nara period nobles]] [[Category:Nara period scholars]] [[Category:Japanese Chinese poets]] [[Category:8th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:7th-century births]] [[Category:8th-century deaths]]
[[ja:下毛野虫麻呂]] ```

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|en|ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|en|ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|en|ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: [[Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|正六位上]] * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|従五位下]]. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|従五位上]]. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}
== References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}
== References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}
== References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. '''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}
== References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: attributing page creation to myself as per first edit summary
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}
== References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}
== References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.

Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

== References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.

Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}
== References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|従五位上}}" to "|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}"
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}
== References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

== References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|正六位上}}" to "|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}}"
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}
== References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}
== References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), qqth month, qqth day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}.
