'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), qqth month, qqth day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), qqth month, qqth day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.

Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.

He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}<references /> == References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=draft}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|12=draft}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇|12=draft}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|12=draft}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=draft}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集|12=draft}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|12=draft}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|12=draft}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|12=draft}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.

Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|12=draft}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=draft}}.

{{draft categories| [[Category:Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Nara period nobles]] [[Category:Nara period scholars]] [[Category:Japanese Chinese poets]] [[Category:8th-century Japanese poets]] [[Category:7th-century births]] [[Category:8th-century deaths]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:下毛野虫麻呂]] [[sn:Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro]] [[sn:himotsukeno no Mushimaro]]

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=draft}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|12=draft}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇|12=draft}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|12=draft}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=draft}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集|12=draft}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|12=draft}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|12=draft}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|12=draft}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|12=draft}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=draft}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}<references /> == References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=draft}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫|12=draft}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|12=draft}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|12=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430. <!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro-->

revision.text:
'''下毛野 虫麻呂'''（しもつけの の むしまろ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[奈良時代]]前紀の[[貴族]]・[[文人]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[朝臣]]。[[参議]]・[[下毛野古麻呂]]の近親か。[[官位]]は[[従五位|従五位上]]・[[式部省|式部員外少輔]]。
== 経歴 == [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）[[従五位|従五位下]]に[[叙爵]]する。翌養老5年（[[721年]]）[[呉粛胡明]]とともに従五位上に昇叙。さらに同月に[[元正天皇]]が官人の中から学業に優れ人々の師範たるに堪える者を選んで褒賞を与えた際、[[文章道|文章]]に優れるとして、[[山田三方]]・[[紀清人]]・[[高丘河内|楽浪河内]]とともに、[[絁]]15疋・[[絹糸]]15絇・[[麻織物|麻布]]30端・[[鍬]]20口を与えられている。同年6月には[[式部省|式部員外少輔]]に任官している。
このほか、『[[経国集]]』に[[対策]]文が2つ収録されている。また、『[[懐風藻]]』に[[長屋王]]の邸宅で行われた[[新羅]]の客を送る宴で詠まれた[[漢詩]]作品1首（五言[[律詩]]）が序文とともに採録されているが、その目次によると[[官位]]は従五位下・大学助教で、年三十六とある。36歳で没したと見られる。
== 官歴 == 注記のないものは『[[続日本紀]]』による。 * 時期不詳：[[正六位|正六位上]] * [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）正月11日：[[従五位|従五位下]]。秋：見[[大学寮|大学助教]]<ref>『懐風藻』</ref> * [[養老]]5年（[[721年]]）正月5日：従五位上。正月27日：賜[[絁]]15疋・[[絹糸]]15絇・[[麻織物|麻布]]30端・[[鍬]]20口。6月26日：[[式部省|式部員外少輔]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *『続日本紀 2 （新日本古典文学大系13）』[[岩波書店]]、1990年 *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年、ISBN 4061590308 *[[江口孝夫]]『懐風藻 全訳注』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、2000年 * [[坂本太郎 (歴史学者)|坂本太郎]]・[[平野邦雄]]監修『日本古代氏族人名辞典』[[吉川弘文館]]、1990年、ISBN 4642022430
{{DEFAULTSORT:しもつけの の むしまろ}} [[Category:下毛野氏|むしまろ]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:奈良時代の学者]] [[Category:日本の漢詩人]] [[Category:8世紀日本の詩人]] [[Category:7世紀生]] [[Category:8世紀没]]

<!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro-->

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His Rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja||ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja||ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}<references /> == References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|ja|経国集|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keikokushū|qq=}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|qq=}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silla|qq=}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|ja|懐風藻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaifūsō|qq=}}'' along with its preface. '''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His Rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goso Kumohime|qq=}}. During the same month, [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|qq=}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|qq=}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''[[:en:Keikokushū|Keikokushū]]''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|qq=}} to bid farewell to guests from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] was included in the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lecturer at the Imperial University|qq=}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=}}.

Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lecturer at the Imperial University|qq=}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=}}.

revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His Rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goso Kumohime|qq=}}. During the same month, [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|qq=}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|qq=}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''[[:en:Keikokushū|Keikokushū]]''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|qq=}} to bid farewell to guests from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] was included in the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lecturer at the Imperial University|qq=}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}<references /> == References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eguchi Takao|qq=}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

[[ja:下毛野虫麻呂]] [[sn:Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro]] [[sn:himotsukeno no Mushimaro]]
<!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--> [[Category:下毛野氏]] [[Category:奈良時代の学者]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:Kanshi poets]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His Rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goso Kumohime|qq=}}. During the same month, [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|qq=}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''[[:en:Keikokushū|Keikokushū]]''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] was included in the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lecturer at the Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}<references /> == References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eguchi Takao|qq=}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, a [[Sangi|councilor]]. His Rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goso Kumohime|qq=}}. During the same month, [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|qq=}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''[[:en:Keikokushū|Keikokushū]]''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] was included in the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lecturer at the Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}<references /> == References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eguchi Takao|qq=}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goso Kumohime|qq=}}. During the same month, [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|qq=}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goso Kumohime|qq=}}. During the same month, [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|qq=}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[:en:silk thread|silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''[[:en:Keikokushū|Keikokushū]]''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] was included in the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.

Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lecturer at the Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

revision.text:
'''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|councilor]]. His Rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goso Kumohime|qq=}}. During the same month, [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|qq=}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[:en:silk thread|silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''[[:en:Keikokushū|Keikokushū]]''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] was included in the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lecturer at the Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goso Kumohime|qq=}}. During the same month, [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|qq=}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|qq=}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[:en:silk thread|silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''[[:en:Keikokushū|Keikokushū]]''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] was included in the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.

His Rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goso Kumohime|qq=|1=Goso Kumohime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65251981}}. During the same month, [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|qq=|1=Yamada Mikata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470140}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|qq=|1=Ki no Kiyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605083}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内|1=Takaoka no Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225788}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[:en:silk thread|silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''[[:en:Keikokushū|Keikokushū]]''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] was included in the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lecturer at the Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}<references /> == References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eguchi Takao|qq=|1=Takao Eguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45830624}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=|1=Kunio Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483736}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.
