<!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--><!--Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro--> [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Scholars of the Nara period]] [[Category:Aristocrats of the Nara period]] [[Category:Kanshi poets]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q65248058]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|councilor]]. His Rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goso Kumohime|qq=|1=Goso Kumohime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65251981}}. During the same month, [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|qq=|1=Yamada Mikata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470140}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|qq=|1=Ki no Kiyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605083}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内|1=Takaoka no Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225788}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[:en:silk thread|silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''[[:en:Keikokushū|Keikokushū]]''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] was included in the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lecturer at the Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}<references /> == References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eguchi Takao|qq=|1=Takao Eguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45830624}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=|1=Kunio Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483736}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

== Notes == {{footnotes}}<references /> == References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eguchi Takao|qq=|1=Takao Eguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45830624}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=|1=Kunio Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483736}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

revision.text:
{{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q65248058]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|councilor]]. His Rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goso Kumohime|qq=|1=Goso Kumohime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65251981}}. During the same month, [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|qq=|1=Yamada Mikata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470140}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|qq=|1=Ki no Kiyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605083}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内|1=Takaoka no Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225788}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[:en:silk thread|silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''[[:en:Keikokushū|Keikokushū]]''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] was included in the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lecturer at the Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}<references /> == References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eguchi Takao|qq=|1=Takao Eguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45830624}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|qq=|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|qq=|1=Kunio Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483736}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|qq=|1=Yamada Mikata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470140}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|qq=|1=Ki no Kiyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605083}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内|1=Takaoka no Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225788}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[:en:silk thread|silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''[[:en:Keikokushū|Keikokushū]]''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] was included in the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.

His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|councilor]]. His Rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goso Kumohime|qq=|1=Goso Kumohime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65251981}}. During the same month, [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|qq=|1=Yamada Mikata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470140}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|qq=|1=Ki no Kiyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605083}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内|1=Takaoka no Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225788}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[:en:silk thread|silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''[[:en:Keikokushū|Keikokushū]]''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] was included in the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lecturer at the Imperial University|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野虫麻呂]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q65248058]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|councilor]]. His Rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goso Kumohime|1=Goso Kumohime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65251981}}. During the same month, [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|1=Yamada Mikata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470140}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|1=Ki no Kiyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605083}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内|1=Takaoka no Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225788}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[:en:silk thread|silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''[[:en:Keikokushū|Keikokushū]]''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] was included in the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}<references /> == References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eguchi Takao|1=Takao Eguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45830624}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|1=Kunio Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483736}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野虫麻呂]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q65248058]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|councilor]]. His Rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goso Kumohime|1=Goso Kumohime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65251981}}. During the same month, [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|1=Yamada Mikata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470140}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|1=Ki no Kiyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605083}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内|1=Takaoka no Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225788}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[:en:silk thread|silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''[[:en:Keikokushū|Keikokushū]]''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] was included in the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野虫麻呂]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q65248058]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|councilor]]. His Rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goso Kumohime|1=Goso Kumohime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65251981}}. During the same month, [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|1=Yamada Mikata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470140}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|1=Ki no Kiyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605083}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内|1=Takaoka no Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225788}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[:en:silk thread|silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''[[:en:Keikokushū|Keikokushū]]''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] was included in the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''[[:en:Keikokushū|Keikokushū]]''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] was included in the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野虫麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野虫麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野虫麻呂]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q65248058]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, a [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|councilor]]. His Rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goso Kumohime|1=Goso Kumohime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65251981}}. During the same month, [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|1=Yamada Mikata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470140}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|1=Ki no Kiyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605083}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内|1=Takaoka no Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225788}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[:en:silk thread|silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''[[:en:Keikokushū|Keikokushū]]''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] was included in the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.

He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|1=Yamada Mikata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470140}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|1=Ki no Kiyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605083}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内|1=Takaoka no Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225788}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[:en:silk thread|silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''[[:en:Keikokushū|Keikokushū]]''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] was included in the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.

The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goso Kumohime|1=Goso Kumohime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65251981}}. During the same month, [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|1=Yamada Mikata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470140}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|1=Ki no Kiyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605083}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内|1=Takaoka no Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225788}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[:en:silk thread|silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''[[:en:Keikokushū|Keikokushū]]''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from [[:en:Silla|Silla]] was included in the ''[[:en:Kaifūsō|Kaifūsō]]'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}<references /> == References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', [[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]] (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eguchi Takao|1=Takao Eguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45830624}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|1=Kunio Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483736}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|1=Yamada Mikata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470140}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|1=Ki no Kiyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605083}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内|1=Takaoka no Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225788}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[:en:silk thread|silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野虫麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野虫麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野虫麻呂]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65248058]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, a {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=councilor}}. His Rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goso Kumohime|1=Goso Kumohime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65251981}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|1=Yamada Mikata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470140}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|1=Ki no Kiyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605083}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内|1=Takaoka no Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225788}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[:en:silk thread|silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|en|Keikokushū|WD=Q870854|lt=Keikokushū}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|en|Kaifūsō|WD=Q2334083|lt=Kaifūsō}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}<references /> == References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|WD=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten}}, 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', {{ill|Kodansha|en|Kodansha|WD=Q726081|lt=Kodansha}} (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eguchi Takao|1=Takao Eguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45830624}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|1=Kunio Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483736}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

== Notes == {{footnotes}}<references /> == References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|WD=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten}}, 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', {{ill|Kodansha|en|Kodansha|WD=Q726081|lt=Kodansha}} (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eguchi Takao|1=Takao Eguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45830624}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|1=Kunio Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483736}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野虫麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野虫麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野虫麻呂]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}}} '''Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro''' (下毛野 虫麻呂, dates unknown) was a {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} noble and [[literary figure]]. His [[kabane|clan name]] was [[Asomi]]. He was likely a close relative of {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, a {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=councilor|1=Sangi (Japan)}}. His Rank was {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}, and he held the post of {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Career == In [[Yōrō 4]] (720), Mushimaro was appointed to the Rank of {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}}. The following year, in [[Yōrō 5]] (721), he was promoted to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to JA}} along with {{ill|Goso Kumohime|ja|呉粛胡明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goso Kumohime|1=Q65251981|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65251981}}. During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō|1=Empress Genshō}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|1=Q11470140|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470140}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|1=Ki no Kiyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605083}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内|1=Takaoka no Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225788}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[:en:silk thread|silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|en|Keikokushū|WD=Q870854|lt=Keikokushū|1=Keikokushū}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|en|Kaifūsō|WD=Q2334083|lt=Kaifūsō|1=Kaifūsō}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Notes == {{footnotes}}<references /> == References == * ''Shoku Nihongi 2'' (Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13), {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|WD=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten|1=Iwanami Shoten}}, 1990. * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, ''Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)'', {{ill|Kodansha|en|Kodansha|WD=Q726081|lt=Kodansha|1=Kodansha}} (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 1992, ISBN 4061590308. * {{ill|Eguchi Takao|ja|江口孝夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eguchi Takao|1=Takao Eguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q45830624}}, ''Complete Commentary on Kaifūsō'', Kodansha (Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko), 2000. * {{ill|Sakamoto Tarō|ja|坂本太郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto Tarō|1=Sakamoto Tarō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425251}}, edited by {{ill|Hirano Kunio|ja|平野邦雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirano Kunio|1=Kunio Hirano|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483736}}, ''Dictionary of Japanese Ancient Clan Names'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 1990, ISBN 4642022430.

During the same month, {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō|1=Empress Genshō}} rewarded officials who demonstrated exceptional academic ability, and Mushimaro was recognized for his literary excellence. He, along with {{ill|Yamada Mikata|ja|山田三方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamada Mikata|1=Q11470140|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470140}}, {{ill|Ki Kiyohito|ja|紀清人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Kiyohito|1=Ki no Kiyoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11605083}}, and {{ill|Takao no Kawachi|ja|楽浪河内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takao no Kawachi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高丘河内|1=Takaoka no Kawachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225788}}, received 15 bolts of silk, 15 skeins of [[:en:silk thread|silk thread]], 30 rolls of [[hemp cloth]], and 20 iron hoes. In June of the same year, he was appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Mushimaro’s works include two policy responses compiled in the ''{{ill|Keikokushū|en|Keikokushū|WD=Q870854|lt=Keikokushū|1=Keikokushū}}''. Additionally, a five-line regulated verse poem (律詩) written during a banquet at {{ill|Prince Nagaya's residence|ja|長屋王邸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nagaya's residence|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to bid farewell to guests from {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}} was included in the ''{{ill|Kaifūsō|en|Kaifūsō|WD=Q2334083|lt=Kaifūsō|1=Kaifūsō}}'' along with its preface. According to the ''Kaifūsō'' index, his Rank at the time was Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and he served as a university lecturer (大学助教). He is believed to have passed away at the age of 36.
== Official Positions == Details are primarily from the ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'' unless otherwise noted: * Date unknown: {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * [[Yōrō 4]] (720), 11th month, 11th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. Autumn: Appointed {{ill|Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja|大学助教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lecturer at the Imperial University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>''Kaifūsō''</ref> * [[Yōrō 5]] (721), 1st month, 5th day: {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. 1st month, 27th day: Received silk, silk thread, hemp cloth, and hoes. 6th month, 26th day: Appointed {{ill|Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja|式部員外少輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Assistant Minister of the Ministry of Ceremonies|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
